meta_title: For Founders: idea validation to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to idea validation and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Idea Validation to Solve Project Stall
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn professional idea validation techniques using Antigravity and Claude Code to ensure your next SaaS build reaches revenue.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of market validation, not a lack of coding skill. To stop stalling, treat your MVP as a revenue-generating experiment. By using Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative refinement, you pivot from 'building features' to 'solving high-value problems' immediately.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: the initial dopamine hit of a new repository, the clean architecture, the ambition. But three weeks in, the momentum dies. Your code becomes a graveyard of half-baked features and 'maybe someday' endpoints. You are not failing because you can't code; you are failing because you are building in a vacuum.

Real founders don't finish projects—they finish businesses. The bridge between a dormant GitHub repo and a profitable SaaS is rigorous validation. By shifting your focus from lines of code to market feedback loops, you transform your build process from a creative drain into a systematic, revenue-generating machine.

steps.title: Define the High-Value Problem
steps.description: Before writing code, define the specific, painful problem your user faces. If you cannot describe the pain in one sentence, you aren't ready to build.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Review this problem statement: [Insert Problem]. Identify the specific persona that would pay $50/mo to solve this and list 3 potential 'must-have' features vs 'nice-to-have' features.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Analyze the business viability of [Problem] and suggest a lean MVP scope.'

steps.title: Build the Landing Page Prototype
steps.description: Validate demand before building the backend. Create a landing page that sells the vision and collects emails. Use Antigravity to scaffold a high-converting waitlist.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a modern, conversion-optimized landing page using Tailwind CSS. Include a hero section, a 'how it works' section, and an email capture form that hooks into a spreadsheet.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --path /src/components/LandingPage.tsx --prompt 'Create a landing page component that emphasizes the core value proposition.'

steps.title: Implement the Core Utility
steps.description: Do not build a CRUD app. Build the single 'Aha!' moment that solves the user's problem. Keep the tech stack lean.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular function that performs [Core Action]. Ensure it is decoupled from the UI and handles error states gracefully.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the business logic for [Core Action] and write unit tests to ensure it succeeds 99% of the time.'

steps.title: Automate the Feedback Loop
steps.description: Inject analytics into your MVP. You need to know exactly where users drop off to validate your assumptions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate a lightweight event tracking system that logs user interaction with the core feature to a structured JSON log file.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --path /lib/analytics.ts --prompt 'Add event tracking to all button clicks and page transitions.'

steps.title: Iterate Based on Real Data
steps.description: Review your logs. If nobody is using the feature, pivot the feature or the target audience. Do not add more code until the current code finds a user.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze these logs: [Paste Logs]. Suggest 3 optimizations to increase user retention for the core feature.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the existing code based on the feedback: [User Feedback] to improve conversion.'

pro_tips: Never start a project with authentication; start with the core value and add auth only when users ask for it.
If your MVP takes more than 14 days to build, you are building too much; cut 50% of the scope.
Use Claude Code to write your README first; if you can't write the documentation, you don't understand the product.
Spend 80% of your time on distribution (talking to users) and 20% on development.
Treat your code as disposable; if it doesn't get traction, delete it and start the next experiment.
content.next_steps: After completing this validation cycle, push your landing page to a live domain and commit to getting your first 10 users before writing a single line of secondary feature code.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping products that people actually want to pay for. Leverage the power of AI to validate, build, and scale faster than ever before. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
